title: Hieracium munduliforme
taxonomy: Hieracium munduliforme is classified as a species within the genus Hieracium, which is part of the family Asteraceae. This classification places Hieracium munduliforme within the group of dandelion-like plants, often referred to as hawkweeds. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Within the genus Hieracium, Hieracium munduliforme is specifically classified as a species within the subgenus Pilosella. This classification system is used to organize and categorize the vast diversity of plant species.

intro: Hieracium munduliforme, also known as Hieracium, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion and hawkweed family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium include sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and dihydroxyalantolactone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hieracium is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
